title: Learning Robust Penetration Testing Policies under Partial Observability: A systematic evaluation
abstract: arXiv:2509.20008v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Penetration testing, the simulation of cyberattacks to identify security vulnerabilities, presents a sequential decision-making problem well-suited for reinforcement learning (RL) automation. Like many applications of RL to real-world problems, partial observability presents a major challenge, as it invalidates the Markov property present in Markov Decision Processes (MDPs). Partially Observable MDPs require history aggregation or belief state estimation to learn successful policies. We investigate stochastic, partially observable penetration testing scenarios over host networks of varying size, aiming to better reflect real-world complexity through more challenging and representative benchmarks. This approach leads to the development of more robust and transferable policies, which are crucial for ensuring reliable performance across diverse and unpredictable real-world environments. translated_title: 在部分可观测性下学习鲁棒的渗透测试策略：一项系统评估
translated_abstract: 渗透测试，即模拟网络攻击以识别安全漏洞，提出了一个非常适合强化学习自动化的序贯决策问题。如同强化学习应用于现实世界问题的许多场景一样，部分可观测性构成了一个主要挑战，因为它破坏了马尔可夫决策过程中存在的马尔可夫性质。部分可观测的马尔可夫决策过程需要历史聚合或信念状态估计来学习成功的策略。我们研究了在不同规模主机网络上的随机、部分可观测的渗透测试场景，旨在通过更具挑战性和代表性的基准来更好地反映现实世界的复杂性。这种方法有助于开发更鲁棒且可迁移的策略，这对于确保在多样且不可预测的现实环境中实现可靠性能至关重要。
tldr: 本研究系统评估了在部分可观测性下，使用标准强化学习方法（如PPO）学习鲁棒且可迁移的渗透测试策略，并通过更具挑战性的网络基准来提升现实适用性。
en_tdlr: This study systematically evaluates learning robust and transferable penetration testing policies under partial observability using standard RL methods like PPO, improving real-world applicability through more challenging network benchmarks.
